title: Piston Tape (Sequential Pusher)
description: A row of pistons that fire in sequence, each pushing a block one step at a time. Controlled by a repeater delay chain — the first piston fires, then the next, creating a wave-like push effect. Used in conveyor-style block movers and sequential door mechanisms.

steps.title: Foundation
steps.explanation: 5-wide, 1-deep stone base. Pistons sit at x=0 and x=2; the delay chain runs along the base. The payload block starts at x=1.

steps.title: Pistons and payload
steps.explanation: Piston A at (0,1,0) facing east. Piston B at (2,1,0) facing east. Payload block (stone) at (1,1,0). When A fires it pushes the block to x=2; then B fires (after delay) and pushes it to x=3. The block moves two steps.

steps.title: Trigger button and delay chain
steps.explanation: Button at (4,1,0). Dust at (4,1,0) → repeater at (3,1,0) facing west delay=2 → feeds piston B at x=2. A direct dust at (4,1,0) also runs west to (0,1,0) powering piston A immediately. Piston A fires first (no delay); B fires 2 ticks later — sequential push.
